options.label: Approve the 26 registered upright traces within exact sectors
options.proposed_rule: Accept the existing 26 unique non-crossing same-view line formulas only as bounded Step 10 interpretation rules inside their exact registered owner-view sectors. Endpoints and formulas remain unchanged; no ownership is granted between or beside traces.
options.evidence_effect: No exact source pixel, sample, measurement, physical target, or prior authority changes.
options.interpretation_effect: Promotes only the exact registered traces from candidate interpretation to approved bounded interpretation if Flamestrike selects this option.
dependencies: S10R-005 sector ownership for any area between traces
known_risks: Discrete approved traces alone do not create a surface or continuous field.

options.label: Reject the 26-trace rule
options.proposed_rule: Reject the current same-side nearest trace interpretation and preserve the endpoints only in their prior source/candidate roles. A new trace rule would require a separate revision gate.
options.evidence_effect: No exact source pixel, sample, measurement, physical target, or prior authority changes.
known_risks: C-004 upright transition planning returns to a blocked state.

options.label: Leave blocked
known_risks: The 26 traces remain candidate interpretation and cannot enter Step 11.

options.label: Piecewise ruled strips between adjacent traces
options.proposed_rule: Within each owner-view sector only, linearly interpolate between adjacent approved trace formulas at matching normalized trace parameter t. Bound every strip by its two traces, introduce no new endpoint extremum, and stop at sector boundaries. Top/upright and cross-view joins remain separate dependencies.
options.evidence_effect: No exact source pixel, sample, measurement, physical target, or prior authority changes.
options.interpretation_effect: Creates a candidate unfilled ownership rule for between-trace sectors only.
known_risks: Ruled strips can show slope changes at trace and sector boundaries.
They do not resolve cross-view corner sectors.

options.label: Nearest-trace sector ownership
options.proposed_rule: Assign each source-unowned sector location to the nearest approved trace under the registered owner-view pixel metric, without interpolating between traces. Preserve exact trace formulas and stop at registered sector boundaries.
options.evidence_effect: No exact source pixel, sample, measurement, physical target, or prior authority changes.
options.interpretation_effect: Creates piecewise ownership regions but no smooth or continuous transition.
known_risks: Nearest-trace boundaries are discontinuous and may be unsuitable for a coherent surface.
It still does not solve the top/upright join.

options.label: Leave blocked
known_risks: Space between and beside traces remains unowned; a joined field cannot be specified.

options.label: Dependency-gated normalized ruled transition field
options.proposed_rule: Only after S10R-002 placement, S10R-003 mapping, S10R-004 trace approval, and S10R-005 sector ownership are approved, define an unfilled candidate field between the approved C-003 target inner boundary and placed N3 outer perimeter using normalized radial station v in [0,1]. Use the approved sector rule for angular ownership and require exact agreement at every registered trace boundary. If any boundary disagrees, the field remains blocked.
options.evidence_effect: No exact source pixel, sample, measurement, physical target, or prior authority changes.
options.interpretation_effect: Creates a written candidate field rule only; no field samples, fill, surface, topology, or geometry are generated here.
dependencies: S10R-003 approved option and C-003 target boundary
known_risks: Boundary incompatibility can block the join.
A normalized ruled field can look overly regular if later implemented without visual review.

options.label: No joined field; independently watertight C-004 component
options.proposed_rule: Do not create a continuous transition field. Keep C-004 as an independently watertight interpreted component and require any hidden connection to C-003 to use a later separately approved minimum-overlap rule in source-invisible volume.
options.evidence_effect: No exact source pixel, sample, measurement, physical target, or prior authority changes.
options.interpretation_effect: Avoids a joined field but introduces a separate hidden-interface and topology dependency.
known_risks: Independent overlap may not match visible transition rhythm.
It defers rather than resolves hidden-interface topology.

options.label: Leave blocked
known_risks: No continuous C-004 transition authority exists; Step 10 cannot close and Step 11 remains blocked.

flamestrike_output_decision.statement: I approve all 7 recommended decisions exactly as listed
